spec_match.rationale: Registers a real SW via Blob URL, the SW responds to `message` with the result of `self.clients.matchAll({includeUncontrolled: true})`. Multiple srcdoc frames can be added and the resulting client list is rendered as a tree.

interactive.rationale: Three-button workflow (register, add frame, list clients) drives a real round-trip — the SW posts back the real `clients.matchAll` result and the page renders each client's URL and type.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises spinning up multiple srcdoc iframes and asking the SW for `clients.matchAll()` — each frame shows up as controlled or isolated. Delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show what happens with `includeUncontrolled: false` vs true, nor with srcdoc frames added before the SW activates. Also the `inh` check (`c.url.includes(location.pathname)`) is a substring match that could give false positives.

references.rationale: See-also links to the correct ChromeStatus entry, https://chromestatus.com/feature/5128675425779712.

design_system.rationale: Page styles use CSS variables, but the injected srcdoc iframe body inlines `background:#f4f3ee` raw hex. The injected HTML escapes the design system, but a `var(--bg-paper)` reference would still resolve if the iframe loads styles. Minor — injected iframes don't share parent stylesheets.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate includeUncontrolled distinction
openQuestions.detail: Add a toggle for `includeUncontrolled: true/false` in the matchAll call. When false, only controlled clients show; the srcdoc inheritance change shifts what counts as `controlled`. That's the most precise way to show the spec change.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the `inh` (inherited) check
openQuestions.detail: Current check is `(c.url||'').includes(location.pathname)`. For deep paths or short pathnames, this could match unrelated URLs. Use `new URL(c.url, location.href).pathname === location.pathname` or compare against `reg.scope`.

openQuestions.title: Add an unregister cleanup
openQuestions.detail: Each Blob URL creates a new SW registration. Add a teardown that unregisters before re-registering, so repeated demo runs don't accumulate registrations.

summary: Best wired of the four concepts — real SW, real matchAll round-trip, real srcdoc iframes. The ChromeStatus ID now matches the feature; remaining follow-ups are precision and cleanup improvements.
